source: 养老机构护理日志真实案例（百度文库/道客巴巴提取）
record_text:
【全日护理日志】王淑兰，83岁，床号305-2。基础疾病：高血压三级、阿尔茨海默病中度（MMSE 12分）、右侧髋关节置换术后2年。
晨间护理（6:30-8:00）：6:30入房观察老人仰卧，呼吸平稳。轻唤后老人睁眼但未应答，目光游离，符合认知障碍表现。协助口腔清洁、洗脸、梳头。生命体征：T36.5℃，BP135/82mmHg，P78次/分，R18次/分。7:30早餐：小米粥一碗、鸡蛋一个、馒头半个，饮水约150ml，进食顺利。
日间护理（8:00-17:00）：协助如厕3次，小便量正常色清。10:00参加院内手指操活动20分钟。12:00午餐：软米饭半碗、清蒸鱼、炒青菜，进食量约正常70%。12:40-14:10午休，睡眠平稳。15:00测BP132/80mmHg，按时服用硝苯地平缓释片1片。
晚间护理（17:00-20:00）：17:30晚餐面条一小碗。19:00温水擦浴，全身皮肤检查：完整无破损、无压红，骶尾部正常。20:00就寝，床栏拉起，呼叫铃置于手边。

source: 养老机构护理记录书写规范范例（百度文库）
record_text: 【新入住记录】长者轮椅入住，步态不稳需搀扶。测T:36.2℃，BP:148/86mmHg，P:82次/分，R:19次/分，SpO₂:96%。意识清醒，对答切题。带入尿管（16Fr，固定妥善）。查体：骶尾部有2×2cm陈旧性色素沉着，四肢肌力：左上肢4级、左下肢3级、右侧肢体5级。既往史：脑梗死后遗症（左侧偏瘫）、高血压病3级、2型糖尿病。遵医嘱予一级护理，已做入住及防跌倒/坠床/防压疮/防脱管压管宣教，长者及家属已知晓。
follow_detail: 新入住72小时重点观察期

source: 养老机构护理记录书写规范范例（百度文库）
record_text: 【压疮护理记录】长者骶尾部有3cm×2cm II期压疮，基底75%红色、25%黄色腐肉，少量黄色渗液。处理：生理盐水清洗创面，水胶体敷料覆盖。挂栏标注：伤口敷料每3日更换。骶尾部色素沉着，全身皮肤干燥脱屑。压疮风险评估为16分（高风险），护理措施：每2小时翻身拍背，气垫床持续使用，赛肤润外涂皮肤保护，保持皮肤清洁干燥，动作轻柔。
follow_detail: 每周评估压疮进展，拍照留档

source: 养老机构护理记录书写规范范例（百度文库）
record_text: 【防压疮记录】长者骶尾部色素沉着，全身皮肤干燥脱屑，压疮风险评估为14分。指导护理员协助长者每2小时翻身拍背，予上气垫床，予赛肤润外涂皮肤，避免长时间受压，保护皮肤清洁，动作轻柔。肛周皮肤潮红，部分皮肤泛白，注意保持肛周清洁干燥，护理时动作轻柔。

source: 养老机构交接班记录真实案例
record_text: 【生活护理交班记录】日期：2015年5月7日。姓名：刘丽，房号/床位：6号房12床，护理等级：二级。医生检查：身体无外伤。喂养方式：普食。陪同家属：女儿王鑫。老人描述：老人因中风后遗症，导致下肢右侧行动不良，拄拐杖能缓慢行走。老人爱美、爱干净，要求每周洗澡两次，每月剪发一次。交班注意事项：1)注意如厕安全，使用助行器 2)每周二/五安排洗澡 3)关注右侧下肢活动情况。
follow_detail: 每周二/五洗澡，关注右侧肢体

source: 养老机构交接班记录真实案例
record_text: 【请假离院/返院记录】离院：2015年10月1日早上9:00，刘坤老人的儿子刘小坤来院接父亲回家团圆，说明2015年10月6日送回。离开时老人神情开朗，身体正常，无不适症状。返院：2015年10月6日13:30老人返回，由刘小坤搀扶进入，身体正常。经检查皮肤无红肿破损，情绪平稳。家属反馈：在家中饮食睡眠正常，未发生意外。

source: 养老机构卧床老人日常照料记录单真实案例
record_text: 【卧床老人日常照料记录】日期：2024年3月。老人情况：卧床不起，需全护理。洗漱护理：协助完成口腔护理、洗脸。内务整理：更换床单。服装换洗：更换睡衣。饮食：喂饭（软食，进食速度慢但仍能完成全量）。如厕：使用尿片，每2-3小时检查更换。翻身：Q2h按时翻身，左右侧卧交替。皮肤情况：完好，骶尾部无发红。睡眠：夜间入睡困难，凌晨2点醒后需安抚。情绪观察：白天平稳，夜间偶有焦虑。备注：已通知医生评估睡眠问题。
follow_detail: 通知医生评估睡眠问题

source: CMS 2567 Deficiency Report — Villa at the Bay (2025)
record_text: 【Critical Incident — Colostomy Neglect Leading to Septic Shock】Resident #3 was transferred to a local hospital by EMS in critical condition after a four-day admission from the facility due to his colostomy not being cared for and resulting in stool contaminating the surgical incision and drains. R3 now had positive blood cultures for VRE (vancomycin-resistant enterococcus) and was in septic shock. Upon examination, R3's abdominal binder that was covering his incision, drains and colostomy bag was off as well as his colostomy bag. R3 was covered in stool. R3's gown was soaked in feces and fluids and his surgical wounds were packed with stool. R3's blood pressure was 66/40. R3 was in septic shock before arriving to the local hospital. R3's PICC line was dirty and dislodged.
follow_detail: Emergency hospitalization, facility cited for neglect

source: CMS 2567 Deficiency Report — South Lafourche Nursing (2019)
record_text: 【Fall Incident — Resident Found on Floor Overnight】Resident reported: 'I fell out of bed last night and screamed all night for someone to help me.' Had dried blood to the upper and lower extremities. Witness statement: 'When I walked in the room, Resident #1 was on the floor, lying in vomit, and there was a round pill in his vomit. Resident #1 told us he had been there all night.' After Resident #1 left, as staff cleaned the blood, it was stuck to the floor and dark in color. Outcome: CNA terminated for neglect; facility failed to report the allegation to the state as required.

【Pressure Sore Deterioration Leading to Hospitalization】A resident was admitted without pressure sores but was identified as being at high risk. By October, the resident was noted to have developed a moderate pressure sore. In mid-November, the resident was transferred to an acute care hospital with a high fever and loss of consciousness resulting from a systemic infection caused by the infected pressure sore. Hospital personnel described him as dry and dehydrated on admission, with a large wound with odorous drainage on the left hip, necrosis on the back of his scrotum, thick purulent drainage from around his catheter, and feces caked on the soles of his feet. One hospital staff person described his condition as a 'picture of neglect.'

【Fall With Delayed Treatment — Fractured Hip】The nursing home failed to obtain prompt treatment for a resident following a fall that resulted in an injury. The resident was not treated and was in pain for 21 hours because the home failed to obtain prompt treatment for a fractured hip. A physical therapist assessed the resident for transfer assistance and determined that the resident needed a mechanical lift for all transfers. When a nurse's aide attempted to manually lift the resident, the resident's leg became caught between the bed rail and the bed, resulting in multiple leg fractures.
follow_detail: 骨折术后康复，21小时延误已立案

source: CQC Inspection Report — Finch Manor Nursing Home (2026)
record_text: 【Medication Management Failure — Covert Administration Without Consent】Covert medication was used for some people without any mental capacity assessment, best-interest decision or documented agreement from relevant professionals. Risk assessments were incomplete or incorrect, and essential tools — such as Waterlow scores, mental capacity assessments, skin integrity plans and behaviour monitoring — were either missing, contradictory, or not updated when people's needs changed. A person who repeatedly expressed a wish to leave the service was deprived of their liberty without a lawful authorisation because an urgent DoLS application had not been submitted.
follow_detail: CQC rated facility Inadequate, enforcement action
